meta.name: 10k Beginner
meta.subtitle: Bridge from 5k to your first 10k
meta.description: For runners who can cover 5km comfortably and want to progress to 10km. Twelve weeks of structured base building with four running days, a weekly long run, and easy-day pace emphasis. No track sessions — the goal is completing the distance, not a time.
meta.coach_notes: You already know you can run. This plan just extends what you can do. The long run on Sunday grows by roughly 1km every two weeks — slow enough to be sustainable, fast enough to arrive at race day ready. Run the easy days genuinely easy.

weeks.title: Base week 1
weeks.note: Week 1 of your first 10k plan. Easy, easy, easy — the pace should feel almost embarrassingly slow on your easy days. You are building aerobic infrastructure this week, not fitness.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Fully conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Pull-ups 3x5 · DB row 3x10 · Push-ups 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first upper body session. Focus on form, not load. Upper body and core strength directly improves your running posture and arm drive efficiency.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km · Middle 3km at a comfortably purposeful pace · Not gasping but not chatting freely — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km easy · Comfortable · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

7km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Slowest pace of the week · This is where the endurance builds — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Base week 2
weeks.note: Week 2 of your first 10k plan. Easy, easy, easy — the pace should feel almost embarrassingly slow on your easy days. You are building aerobic infrastructure this week, not fitness.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Fully conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km · Middle 4km at a comfortably purposeful pace · Not gasping but not chatting freely — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 7km easy · Comfortable · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

8km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Slowest pace of the week · This is where the endurance builds — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Base week 3
weeks.note: Third week and the long run reaches 9km this Sunday — the furthest you have run since starting this plan. Keep the pace very easy. Walk if you need to. The goal is time on feet, not pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Fully conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 7km · Middle 5km at a comfortably purposeful pace · Not gasping but not chatting freely — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 8km easy · Comfortable · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

9km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Slowest pace of the week · This is where the endurance builds — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Deliberate rest week. Volume drops. The adaptations from weeks 1–3 happen now, not during the hard sessions — they happen in the recovery. Trust the process.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km easy · Relaxed — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km easy · No targets — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
7km easy · Comfortable and relaxed — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Development week 1
weeks.note: Back to full training after the deload. You should notice you feel fresher than week 3 — that is the adaptation working. The Thursday run has a steady middle section this week.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km · Middle 4km at steady effort (5:30–5:50/km) · Comfortably challenging — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 7km easy · Light and comfortable — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

9km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Conversational · You are building real endurance now — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Development week 2
weeks.note: Long run reaches 10km this Sunday. That is your target race distance. Run it easy — this is practice for the distance, not the race pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 7km · Middle 5km at steady effort (5:30–5:50/km) · Comfortably challenging — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 8km easy · Light and comfortable — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

10km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Conversational · You are building real endurance now — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Development week 3
weeks.note: Your long run hits 11km this week — further than race distance. If you can run 11km comfortably in training, 10km at a slightly quicker pace on race day is entirely within your reach.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 8km · Middle 6km at steady effort (5:30–5:50/km) · Comfortably challenging — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 9km easy · Light and comfortable — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

11km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Conversational · You are building real endurance now — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Deload week 2
weeks.note: Second deload. Volume drops. You are entering the final phase of the plan — four weeks of race preparation. Rest well this week.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
8km easy · Relaxed and comfortable — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Race prep week 1
weeks.note: Race preparation phase. The Thursday run now includes a sustained effort section at close to race pace — this builds confidence at the speed you will need on race day.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km · 3km easy, 2km at 5.2/km, 1km easy · Building race confidence — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km · Middle 4km at steady effort · Race-pace awareness — A short threshold session to maintain the feel of race effort. Comfortably hard — not racing.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 7km easy · Comfortable — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
10km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Relaxed and comfortable — A confidence long run in taper — resist the urge to push. Arrive at the start line rested, not tired from a final fitness test.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Race prep week 2
weeks.note: Second race prep week. The long run reduces slightly as we approach the race — this is deliberate, not a sign you are doing less. Your legs need to be fresh for race day.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km · 3km easy, 2km at 5.1/km, 1km easy · Building race confidence — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km · Middle 3km at steady effort · Race-pace awareness — A short threshold session to maintain the feel of race effort. Comfortably hard — not racing.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km easy · Comfortable — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
9km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Relaxed and comfortable — A confidence long run in taper — resist the urge to push. Arrive at the start line rested, not tired from a final fitness test.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Race prep week 3
weeks.note: Final training week. Everything this week is gentle. Your fitness is set — it cannot improve in the next 7 days but it can be damaged if you do too much. Trust the work.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 6km · 3km easy, 2km at 5.0/km, 1km easy · Building race confidence — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km · Middle 2km at steady effort · Race-pace awareness — A short threshold session to maintain the feel of race effort. Comfortably hard — not racing.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km easy · Comfortable — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
8km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Relaxed and comfortable — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 minutes before. Hydrate well.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Race week
weeks.note: Race week. Everything serves the Sunday race. Rest, eat well, sleep well. You have trained for twelve weeks — the fitness is built. Your only job now is to arrive at the start line fresh and ready.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 5km easy · 4x100m strides · Keep it light — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 3km easy · Legs moving, nothing hard — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — let the adaptation work.

sessions.det: 3km easy · Very comfortable — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
1km easy warm-up · Race at goal pace · Start conservatively — first 2km should feel easier than you expect · Build from 5km · Finish strong

nut.d: Porridge with banana or toast with peanut butter. 400–500ml water. Only familiar foods.
150–200ml water. Walk to the start line. Trust your training.
Take water at stations if available. Sip, don't gulp. No gels needed for a 10k unless you have practised with them.
Water immediately. 400ml in the first 10 minutes. Banana or protein bar at the finish. Proper meal within 90 minutes — protein and carbohydrates.
You just ran a 10k. That is genuinely impressive. Eat well, rest, and think about what is next.
